item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh boy where do i start

First the choice of language

The Prince talks in old japanese instead being form the heinan period (he should be speaking in early middle japanese instead)

Second the sword

Why call it an odachi? The odachi is from the muromachi period and was curved you should just wrote that he had a two handed sword with two sides with a side note that this is unusual for the period which he came from and then anyone with enough knowledge of japanese history would get why that is thanks to the Tsurugi reference

Third the Yokai

I love Yokai lore and the Yokai are complex entities with different shapes and moral alignments to put all of them under an umbrella of evil being which the Prince needs to exterminate just ignores and cheapens all of the rich lore which they have behind them and i really REALLY dont like that it feels so cheap

It would be better if you just put Oni (which are always portrayed as evil and hamrful) instead of Yokai as a whole

I am really saddened to say this because i really liked the Prince's character and ending but all the other stuff are too much of an elephant in the room for me to ignore

-1 From me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A bit of technical critique, your description of using positron emission tomography to describe the method for identifying Y-class entities doesn't make sense and is described incorrectly. Positron emission tomography requires a radioactive tracer to be injected into the subject, which of course wouldn't work on non-corporeal entities. You may want to choose a different method for identifying the entities.

item.title: Re: Thanks for all who helped!
item.content:encoded: Yes, that's no problem! Sorry for the late response.

item.title: Re: Thanks for all who helped!
item.content:encoded: hey i really like this post an im seeing that your really the only one with Yokai so would you mind if i link this in my other scips that im making wish you the best in making more of these

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A very good article and a interesting reading, the entity is the definition of a Guardian.

+1

item.title: This needs more love.
item.content:encoded:
This is the definition of underrated. I thought that I wouldn't like it because of the low positives, but it was a very interesting read.

You have won my +1 weeeks ago

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, my only concerns are several cases of repeating words in dialogue and the mention of an Ukiyo-e artist; Ukiyo-e did not become a thing until the late 17th century and onwards; I'd just go with the term 'artist'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just to let you know, we have some Yokai in containment such as the Gashadokuro, Kappas, and a Tengu.

Overall, I like the writing of this and the idea of a Yokai hunter. I wish we had a point of view from IJEAMA since I can see them very interested in this guy to capture Yokai.

The position of the dates in the journal excerpts kinda bug me since aren't they suppose to be at the top?

item.title: Thanks for all who helped!
item.content:encoded:
My second SCP! Thank you to all those who critiqued my SCP!
